theScene: Getting a grip on herself, the red slime jumps off her chair with a mix of excitement and trepidation, clearly still fearful of going back into the darkened tunnels despite her genuine thrill at the prospect of the impending adventure. Leaving you to get ready on your own, she makes her preparations, carefully packing up her map, notes, and more before the two of you make your way out of her quirkily head-shaped home.

As you walk out of the village up to the nearby checkpoint gem, you get a number of passing waves and cheers from nearby blue slimes, as well as from a harpy resting from a recent delivery, the avian monster girl seeming happy to join in on the merry send-off.
Go Petua and [ThePlayerName], our heroes! Yay!
Don't get lost again, Petua! Have fun!
I-I'm not gonna get lost! And you have fun too- with whatever you do-

The offending blue slime just lets out a bubbly giggle in response, while all around you, well wishes keep ringing out from more and more Sleim Vllgers drawn by the hubbub, their confused praise getting abruptly cut off as you and 'Petua' touch the checkpoint gem.

Instantly, you find yourselves back in the dark depths of the Cavern tunnels right by the Labyrinth's entrance, the stark change of scenery along with the sudden rush of not-so-stellar memories making the red slime shiver in skittish distaste as she looks about warily before getting a grip on herself and focusing on the matter at hand, grabbing her heavy book to leaf through it while thinking aloud.

Alright... Take this tunnel, then this one, then this one... Keep going, making sure to stick to the right-hand wall... And from there, we should just need to go that way and we'll have reached the place {i}without running into Amy{/i}.

The enterprising goo girl speaks with renewed determination, authoritatively grabbing your hand to take you down one of the many nearby tunnels without further ado. You suspect that she may not even have realized her gesture, but seeing no reason to correct her, you let her lead you on while she uses her free hand to hold on to her strange doodly map, which she consults every now and then with a thoughtful mutter.

Time slips by as your expedition leader makes her way down the many twists and turns of the Caverns' unfathomable depths, clearly confident in her pathfinding judging by her focused expression and staunch smile.

All the same, the trek sure is taking a {i}long{/i} time, hour after hour passing until, all of a sudden, Perpetua eventually stops at a crossroads, staring pensively at the yawning corridors around you, then back at her map, then at the pathways again, and back again, before finally looking over to you with a big smile.

Her blank smile stays stuck to her face as she does her best to casually brush off {i}landing you both potentially in the middle of nowhere{/i}, but seeing the expression you're making in response to her brazen confession, she hastily pipes up again before you can say anything.
{b}B-But!{/b} I'm {i}pretty sure{/i} we're in the right area! We just need to-

Just as she goes to move into one of the tunnels seemingly at random, gems light up the hallway all at once, suddenly filling the surroundings with light while a ghostly visage reveals itself right in front of the cherry slime, mere inches from her face!

........ A downright awkward silence follows the abrupt apparition that Perpetua somehow barely reacts to, just blinking in very mild shock before looking over to you with a joyful grin, triumphantly pointing at the jumpscaring ghost with a gooey finger.
See? I told you we were close!
Buguwah- Y-You didn't even get a little scared---?!
Ghosts aren't scary, silly. Anyways, where're the other two?

While the first ghost pouts at Perpetua, mumbling something about being 'such a spoilsport', two more pale spectral women drift out from nearby tunnels, one looking just as discomfited as the first while the third chuckles good-humoredly.
Bleh, you're no fun. We wanted to at least spook you a bit first...

While your cherry-colored companion makes a show of emphatically rolling her eyes at the ghosts' antics, the third one breezily loops through the air right next to you with a casual smile, looking you up and down with keen interest... and the kind of {i}eager{/i} expression you've grown accustomed to seeing on monster girls' faces.
But say, who's your friend, Perp? Is he your-
The trio lets out a chorus of curious 'Ooooh's with varying degrees of spookiness and horniness at this information, each giving you a smile and a wave as they happily introduce themselves in turn.
And if you really want us to join your little village... {size=-5}{i}Which I assume is what you're here for since you kept bringing it up the last time we met...{/i}{/size}
You're going to need to answer our riddles...
Wh- The heck!? Last time, it was {i}three{/i}! You can't just change the deal!
{i}Well{/i}... you brought a friend along, so it's only fair that we increase the number of riddles proportionally. Otherwise, that's kinda sorta pretty much cheating, hm?
Perpetua harrumphs at the explanation, but as she can't exactly argue against the logic, she just sulks silently while a giggling Virosa pipes up again.

But mind youuuu... We won't be expecting you to get {i}all{/i} of them right. As long as you can answer {i}most{/i} of them correctly, we shall be satisfied. So don't let your mistakes haunt you toooOooO much, hihihi~

The seductive ghost concludes her explanation with what sounds like her best impression of a witchy cackle, the ectoplasmic goo girl promptly joined by her two sisters whose not-so-foreboding giggles fill the tunnel you're standing in, until Perpetua stops them by waving her arms imperiously.
{i}Alright, alright!{/i} ...But for your information, though, everything's already set up back at the village for you guys to move in, soooo... Just saying all we'd need is your go-ahead.♪
F-For real? Hey girls, that actually sounds like a pretty cushy deal. Maybe we could-
No way! Riddles are {i}sacred{/i}! No riddles, no deal!
Drat... Alright, what's the first riddle, then?

In response, the three ghosts huddle together to speak between themselves in hushed tones, occasionally gesturing, nodding, or shaking their heads vigorously through what appears to be quite the animated debate... until they seem to come to an agreement, turning back towards you with downright ceremonial attitudes.
Aaaaand, in our ghostly mercy, we shall begin by giving you an easy freebie bonus one, just to warm up.

Smoothly proceeding, Mycia and Virosa turn to Amanita who clears her throat with a grin, then floats up to you with a grandiose sway of her arms, the ectoplasmic flames around her flickering showily as she intones dramatically.

Perpetua stares blankly at the 'spooky' ghost woman face as she wiggles her fingers in an effort to look creepier, the silence stretching on somewhat awkwardly until the cherry slime eventually speaks up.
That's... That's not a riddle. That's not even a hard question.
Guh- {i}Okay{/i}, who's asking the riddles here, huh? Not you!
And {i}we{/i} know that {i}you{/i} know the answer, dear Perpetua, so for this one, no assistance: your guest will need to answer on his own, hmmmhihi...~
With Virosa's snickering laughter hanging in the air, Perpetua flatly stares at the grinning trio with a pout before looking to you expectantly.
Form on cave walls like crystals.
Made from water, sugar, gelatin, and air.
Grow in marshlands on marshmallow bushes.

EventText.NameOfScene: Grow in marshlands on marshmallow bushes.
theScene: {b}Correct!{/b} Sooooo... we'll give you oOone bonus point, ooOOoO.
Wow, it's almost like you want to move in and are just being coy about it or something...
N-No we're nooOOOooot- J-Just wait until you taste our head-scratchers and you'll be singing a different tuUuUune! {i}First riddle!{/i}
{i}Buh-{/i} M-Magic can do lots of things- That's not a real answer!
And magically grown marshmallows don't taste right...
While Mycia and Amanita make faces at the notion and consider your response skeptically, Virosa steps in with a kind smile.
Hihi... Since it's not exactly right, but not exactly wrong either, why don't you try again?
Meanwhile, Perpetua merely keeps looking at you in silence, curiously arching a brow in reaction to your choice of answer.

EventText.NameOfScene: Form on cave walls like crystals.
theScene: D-Dang it... Maybe I should have gone over the plan more thoroughly with you...
Hehe, don't feel bad, darlings, you still have six more real riddles to go.
And marshmallows growing like that actually sounds kinda cool!
You still don't get any points, though... But let's move on. {i}First Riddle{/i}!

EventText.NameOfScene: Made from water, sugar, gelatin, and air.
theScene: The three ghosts and Perpetua freeze in place at your answer, staring at you in blank confusion.
That- What-? {i}What{/i} are you talking about, [ThePlayerName]-???
Y-Yeah! That's so wrong I don't even think we can accept that as an answer- Did you make it up-?
Or steal it from a fantasy novel???
Heh, or maybe the education in Fertilia is just really really baaaad...~
Giggling and snickering, Mycia and Virosa hover around you with teasing grimaces, until Amanita brings them in line with a pointed cough.
{i}Anyways!{/i} Try again! To help you out, marshmallows grow from something...
Yes, please do try again, we won't be mean! ...And don't feel bad if you miss it. It's a bonus question, after all.~
Just answer more seriously this time...
{b}Soft, bouncy, squishy, and yummy! What am I?{/b}
All three ghosts stay stuck in place with remarkable synchronicity at Perpetua's response, sporting stupefied expressions at the seemingly unexpected answer.
{i}Nonono{/i}! S-Something that is soft, bouncy, squishy, and yummy, and {i}not{/i} a marshmallow!
Perpetua rolls her eyes at the ectoplasmic woman's not very smooth course-correction, and looks over to you for your answer on the matter while the other two ghosts snicker in amusement at the third.
That- feels very biased... I feel like a lot of other things could fit that.
{i}Hmph.{/i} Well it was {i}my{/i} riddle, so tough beans. When {i}you{/i} make riddles, you can give them the answers you want.
H-Hey, that's not a bad idea! I could use my smarts to stump [ThePlayerName] in a duel of wits!
While Perpetua smirks schemingly, looking like she's already imagining all the ways she could bring you low with her genius, Virosa politely chimes in.
{i}Ahem!{/i} Back to {i}our{/i} riddles, hehe... It's my turn! {i}Riddle two{/i}!
Yeah, I've never heard of that either?
Oh! {i}I{/i} know about it! Silver got a sampler shipped in from kunoichi artisans that one time! It's like this slab of smooth white stuff that's really jiggly!
Hmmm... Is that so... Heh, well I think we have to count that as {b}correct{/b}, then?
Aw... But the answer is supposed to be 'mushroom'...
Defeated, Mycia moodily hovers away from the spotlight with Amanita consolingly patting her back.
There, there, Mycie. There's still more riddles to tell... Virosa, {i}second verse{/i}, if you will?
The trio squawks in sputtering flustered unison at your answer, while Perpetua gives you a skeptical side glance for your daring approach.
Sh-Should we actually accept that-? I-It's not {i}wrong{/i}, but it's kind of weird-???
I-I mean I guess we {i}are{/i} soft, bouncy, squishy, a-and yummy!?... I-It was supposed to be 'mushroom', but I suppose we can take that, so... {b}Correct!?{/b}
While Mycia and Amanita confer animatedly, pinching and poking each other to confirm their softness and the rest, Virosa comes forward with a breezy giggle.
Hehehe, I didn't know Perpetua hanged out with such kinksters.~
I-It's not like that-! People drinking slime blobs isn't {i}that{/i} weird-! I-It's even catching on in Delusian cuisine!

The cherry slime's flustered, defensive response isn't making things better, the two curious ghosts exchanging whispered comments with blushing glances at the two of you while Virosa speaks up with a spooky smile.
Hehe, be that as it may, it's my turn now. {i}Riddle two{/i}!
I mean- Is it really that wrong-?
Y-Yes! The question wasn't sexual! The answer was supposed to be 'mushroom', you perv... {i}And no, not {b}that{/b} kind of 'mushroom'-{/i}! {b}I-Incorrect!{/b}
Hehehe... Well, lewdly wrong answer aside, it's now my turn to tell {i}riddle two{/i}!

As Virosa takes Mycia's place, you can hear Perpetua mutter 'That should have counted...' with a hint of sore loser bitterness while Mycia and Amanita look at the two of you with blushing glances, seeming to be developing quite the opinion of your companionship...
A stunned silence welcomes your response...
... following which all three ghosts sport knowing lascivious expressions while Perpetua squeaks indignantly, her voice choking under the surprise.
I think we have to count that as valid, hehe...
Yeah... Wow... Talk about brazen. It was supposed to be 'mushroom', but I'll agree that's {b}correct{/b}!
Uhuhuh... Man, Perp, you and your boyfri-
While Perpetua keeps spastically rapid-firing in vain at the two ghosts who just look at her with snickering grins, Virosa smoothly takes the stage with a cool smile.
Hehe, well, moving on, it's my turn to tell {i}riddle two{/i}!
Dramatically, Virosa clears her throat, and intones emphatically.

{b}Pitter patter goes the rain, splash and slash go their claws as they scamper and dive. A chorus of hunting howls rings out! Soon you will be left powerless as they milk you dry... Hehe, what is hunting you?{/b}
Hmm... That could be a few things, I think... But it's probably something they're familiar with...
Hehehehe... That is {b}correct{/b}... Perhaps I went too easy on you... Ah well, we'll chalk that up to your charm.♥
Virosa wiggles her hips at you with a rather unsubtly lecherous expression, Perpetua treating her to a narrowed glare before shrugging and turning to you with a beaming smile to exchange a high-five.
Good job, [ThePlayerName]! I {i}think{/i} I coulda figured that one out, but it's all the better to have you around to help.♪
Hehehehe... That is unfortunately {b}incorrect{/b}... Lumirens may dive, but they don't scamper, nor do they have claws or go out in the rain... And it would be unfair to call their song a howl.
Oh, that's too bad... But since it wasn't 100% wrong, can we try again-?
You must have met some very terrifying harpies...
I'll say... Harpies that slash, scamper, and howl? That's actually scary to imagine!

Hm, I guess that {i}could{/i} exist... But for all intents and purposes, that answer is {b}incorrect{/b}... Harpies typically do not hunt in the rain, and neither do they stick to the ground, or howl.
Dangit... W-Well, can we go again, then?
Nnnnope! Can't make it too easy for you guys. Get it right, or no points for you.~
I'm pretty sure that's not right...
There {i}can{/i} be all kinds of spirits with claws or who howl, but they still do not, and cannot, scamper in the rain... That is {b}incorrect{/b}.
Guh- Darn it, can't put one over on you... Though I guess I shouldn't be surprised that given you're {i}ghost slimes{/i}, you know your subject...
Indeed, hehe, it's a fun topic to research.~
That is very {b}incorrect{/b}... No points for you.
{i}Super{/i} incorrect, even! We should give you minus one point for that! Are you even taking the fine tradition of riddles seriously!?
Now, now, Mycie, calm down. I'm sure it was an honest mistake.
While Amanita tempers Mycia's indignant anger, Perpetua takes you aside to speak to you sternly, her attitude reminding you of a coach in the middle of a tournament.
What gives, [ThePlayerName]!? You better not be messing around! Remember, this is serious...

Immediately, the cherry slime jumps with impressive speed to hide under your cover, huddling behind your legs and casting fearful glances around while even Mycia looks rattled and the other two sigh, Virosa seeming to take it upon herself to act as the voice of reason by answering composedly.

{i}Ahem...{/i} Even should Amy be more than an {i}urban legend{/i}, while most of the described traits {i}would{/i} fit her, such as the claws, scampering, diving, and hunting, she does not howl, and there is no 'they' with Amy. There is only one Amy.
B-But she really exists! I told you! I met her once and she chased me for {i}hours{/i}! I barely escaped with my ectoplasm!
A matango even told me that there may be {i}several{/i} Amys, or that she can duplicate herself!
Oh for the love of- Nita, when will you stop listening to these- {i}Anyways!{/i} That is {b}incorrect{/b}, and we shall be moving on now...
And with that said, without skipping a beat, Amanita jumps in, looking mightily pleased to have the floor again.
Alright! Then next would be me for {i}riddle three{/i}! OoOoOOpen your ears wide, for I won't repeat myself!
{i}The Epic of Entropy{/i}, spoken by the Black Knight, on page 275 in reference to...

Perpetua immediately cuts in before the drifting not-quite-spectral woman can even finish half of her sentence, leaving the trio flustered and flabbergasted as the gung-ho red slime goes on to quote the rest of the excerpt verbatim, even carrying on with the ensuing narration up until Amanita stops her with a sputter.
Wait wait {i}{size=+3}wait{/size}{/i}- I wasn't even done yet- That's not fair at all-!
Hm. I would say that, on the contrary, it seems {i}quite fair{/i} that you got played for a fool after you got lazy and just copied an existing riddle.
Hmm... Yeah, Nita, I think she got you on that one...
Hm~hm~hmmm~ Unfortunately for you, you stole that riddle straight from my favorite book. You never had a chance, ohoho.♪
Having clearly dug her own grave, the ghostly slime can just pout ruefully at the swaggering Perpetua, huffily throwing up her arms and looking away while muttering spitefully.
Hrrrmrhr... Fine, that's {b}correct{/b}. Take your point and choke on it, hrmph...
Well then, I guess it's on to the next one already, hehehe...~ Why don't you go again, Nita?

Looking still sore over her loss, but seeming mollified by Virosa's gesture, Amanita gets a grip on herself and does her best to put on a brave face, thinking for a moment before declaring with renewed gusto.
{b}Twisting and turning, darkly perverse and yearning, forever and ever I go... What place am I?{/b}

Seemingly knocked off her pedestal by the foreboding description, Perpetua holds back a shiver and doesn't say anything, looking to you in a mute appeal to your keen decision-making skills. It seems like you can't count on her for guidance this time...
Amanita joyfully claps her hands in congratulations, clearly not hurt that you saw right through her riddle... Probably because she actually got to say it in full this time.
While her sisters cheer her and confer between themselves to decide who will go next, Perpetua lets out a sigh of relief next to you.
Phew- I would have said the Caverns... I didn't even explore the Labyrinth that much, what with having left the hero-chasing business to my faithful servants... So yeah, good job, [ThePlayerName]!
Alright! My turn again this time!
Y-Yeah! It's gotta be the Caverns! They're super big and twisting and dark and-
Nnnnope! That is {b}incorrect{/b}! The answer was: the Labyrinth!
For indeed, what place could be darker, twistier, perverser, turninger, foreverer, and- Er...
While Amanita trails off, tripping over her own words, Mycia gently floats up to Perpetua to comfortingly pat her on the head.
Chin up, Perpetua! Riddles should be enjoyed for their own sake, not just to win.
Yuh-uh! There's two more riddles to go, so don't throw in the shroud yet! And it's my turn again this time!
Whaow... The whole continent? That's quite the answer... And a perspective that only an adventurer could have, huh...
Hm, but still, does it count?
Mhmhmh... I must say I can see the logic there... It {i}is{/i} a pretty kinky and confounding place, and one that certainly has its dark sides, hi hi hi...
Yeah, works for me too! {b}Correct{/b} it is! And now, it's my turn again!
While Perpetua pumps her fist in celebration, Mycia takes a deep breath and recites.
The Capital? It {i}is{/i} huge and confusing and all, but...
While we've never been there ourselves, we've heard quite a bit about it, and we know that calling it 'darkly perverse and yearning' would be... a bit overdramatic.
Mhmh... And it certainly isn't endless, no matter what certain humblebragging voltlins would have you believe.
All in all, that's {b}incorrect{/b}! So no point for you, and now, it's my turn again!
Perpetua grumbles grouchily next to you, likely feeling like that answer should have counted, especially considering her first experience with the place...
Uh... No, I don't think so. That's obviously {b}incorrect{/b}.
While Mycia and Amanita fix you with blank stares and Perpetua fumes behind you, Virosa lets out a prim giggle.
I can't tell if you're trying to make it sound better or {i}worse{/i} to move there, hehe.~
I- Okay- There was {i}no way{/i} that was the right answer! [ThePlayerName], did you hit your head on the way here? Are you okay---?

You feel like you could answer that you didn't hear much help coming from {i}her{/i}... but you also know that bickering wouldn't achieve anything and that you would be better advised to keep your eye on the prize, Mycia precisely coming forward with an enthusiastic competitive grin on her face.
And now, it's my turn again!
Ahem... {b}Wings that flap, a voice that trills, a screech that stuns, an ass that enthralls! Who am I?{/b}
Oh! That's gotta be a harpy, right-?
O-Or maybe not... They don't have stunning screeches or enthralling asses, r-right? ...Ergh, you should answer, [ThePlayerName], not me. I feel like I'd get it wrong...
That's {b}correct{/b}! One even lives in these very Caverns!
Wait, really-?! How come I never met her-?
She probably spooked a scaredy slime like you off.
Hey! I am {i}not{/i} a scaredy slime.
If anything, {i}she{/i} was likely scared by my great and majestic presence.
While Mycia and Amanita chuckle incredulously before the bombastic slime's claim, Virosa actually looks pensive at the idea, and eventually chimes in.
Oh, yes, she {i}is{/i} a scaredy bat. It's very easy to frighten her, mhmhmh...~
Seeing the ghost slime's lecherous expression, you can't help but feel for the poor bat girl, and wonder what kind of tricks and scares Virosa must be putting her through...
Wait, don't they? I know a matango who knows a harpy who knows another harpy who works at the Temple, and she told me they have a tengu there who's a real storm when she gets angry...
Really? ...Sounds like hearsay to me... Hearsay and slander.
H-Hey, {i}I{/i} didn't say it, it was the harpy!
Well... {i}We{/i} don't know, so I'll stick to my judgment.
Hmmm- I can see where you're coming from, but they don't have any kind of stunning screech. Sadly, that's {b}incorrect{/b}.
The lewd ghost's raunchy comment is received with deadpan stares from not only Perpetua but also her sisters, prompting her to move on hastily.
... Isn't that just a harpy dressed up like a tengu-?
No way! I've been told they go through some super serious training to hone their voices into ones capable of hypnotic singing and all other kinds of nifty abilities!
Huh, okay? ... Do they have stunning screeches and enthralling asses, then?
Ah?- Er, n-no, I don't think so?
Hmph, there you have it, then. {b}Incorrect!{/b} Sorry guys.
Y-Yeah, aren't they, like, octopus ladies? I can't imagine one with wings...
That would be quite the sight, though~
For real. I can't tell if that would be more scary, sexy, or amazing... But at any rate, that's {b}incorrect{/b}.
Erm- Maybe {i}some{/i} dragons can do {i}some{/i} of that stuff, but that's not the answer, sorry.
H-Hey, think about it, though: with dragons being so powerful, there's gotta be at least {i}one{/i} that can do all of those things, right?
B-But come on, that's not a real answer! You might as well just say 'A super powerful archmage' and it would fit practically anything! That's just cheating! {b}Incorrect{/b}!
Anyways, with that out of the way, only one more riddle left, and it's mine to tell, huhuhu...~
Seeming to be milking her final time in the spotlight for all it's worth, Virosa sways elegantly in the air, giving a mystical flair to her speech as she recites the last riddle.
{b}The source of life, the law of this land, your joy, your plight, and your downfall. What am I?{/b}
Wh-What? What in the cherries is that even supposed to mean-??? I can't help but think it's something that sounds complicated but is actually super down to earth-!
Clutching her head, Perpetua looks like she has many ideas and possibilities pinballing through her mind, but that none are making the cut. Seems like you'll have to go it alone once more...
Oooooh! I get it now!... Wow, that one was tricky...
Or kinda obvious when you know what a hornball Rosa can be.

The culprit doesn't even try to deny the accusation, just giggling lecherously under the groans and eye rolls of Mycia and Perpetua while Amanita seems to be abstaining from taking sides, instead carrying on in stride.
At any rate, good job! We didn't think you'd get that one!
A good guess, and a poetic one... But I would not consider it the 'law of the land', so that's {b}incorrect{/b}, hehe~
Yeah, methinks the answer is more... prosaic.
Huhu, I shall neither confirm nor deny this~
Gah... Virosa's are always so tricky...
While the ghost slime in question makes it a point to keep her poker face on behind her cheerful grin, Amanita comes forward, treating you to a comforting smile of her own.
Well, don't worry about it. I think you two did great!
Hmmm- While it has play into these lands, and breeds its own ups and downs, magic is not {i}quite{/i} the source of life. {b}Incorrect{/b}!
|f|ChangeImageLayer|/|Expression|/|3|/|Upset|n|... And I thought we said no more magic! That's just a {i}lazy{/i} answer...
I kinda wish I could better use magic, though. I can only cast basic stuff like Analyze and a wimpy Arouse...
Hi hi, well... You know that ghost slimes are quite magically proficient, right? And the secret of that power is... {i}marshmallows{/i}~
Hah! Nice try, Virosa, but it's cherries for life for this gal. You won't get me to buy into your marshmallow craze.

Sensing that this might go on for a while, Amanita steps in before the conversation turns into a whole debate on magically-enhancing diets, sporting a perhaps slightly forced smile as she cheerfully pipes up.
Quite the curious choice... But it is neither the source of life nor the law of these lands, which makes your answer {b}incorrect{/b}, I'm afraid!
And good thing too... Feels like living in a world where technology is everywhere in your life would be scary.
The ghostly trio welcome the assertion with nods and mutters of assent, seeming to be in definite agreement on the subject.

Hmmm... She {i}could{/i} fit, but I do not think she is the cause of your plights or the source of your downfalls in any prevalent way, so that's {b}incorrect{/b}... Unless you buy into some {i}very{/i} farfetched theories.
Yeah, I hear Vernea is actually really nice!
You could at least pronounce her name right. Ve-ne-re-a-e, Mycie... If you're interested in the goddess, we could actually take you to a church one day.
I-I don't want to get exorcized, though!
We don't- Nevermind, forget about it.
I can see your logic, and I {i}rather{/i} like the mystique of it, but I wouldn't say that nature is the 'law' of these lands... {b}Incorrect{/b}! Nice try, though~
Heh, you're playing it cool, but I bet that over the next few days, you'll be spending hours working it into a new riddle.
Sure, and neither did we see you lovingly mull that 'source of life' one over and over until you were happy with it.
Wh-Why am I getting grilled all of a sudden!? L-Let's just move on!
There is a pause following the ghostly banter, the ectoplasmic trio looking at each other thoughtfully as if uncertain of how to proceed, prompting Perpetua to ask.
That was the sixth one, so we're done, right?
Indeed! Time sure flies when you're having fun, doesn't it? Now, to tally your results...
Another short intermission ensues, through which the quizzing sisters mentally review your results, your slime companion sporting an expression of nervous hopefulness as she awaits their verdict.
...W-Wow- Actually, only Perpetua got one right...
M-Maybe we should have taken it easier on them...?
The cherry slime can't mask her disappointment and frustration at the near total failure, looking away dejectedly.

It seems like she doesn't even feel like looking at you. You're not sure whether she's feeling angry, disappointed, or just sad, but before the heavy silence can drag on to the point of becoming hurtful, Virosa chimes in, hovering closer with a knowing smile.
Now now, before you both get into a tiff over it, hehehe... You {i}did{/i} still do your best to overcome our challenge, and I'm sure you had some fun, yes?
Huh- I mean- Yeah, it {i}was{/i} fun, but I came here to get you to join Sleim Vllge, not just 'have fun'...
We- May have already decided we wanted to move in as soon as we got the okay anyway...

But it just felt too {i}easy{/i} and woefully lacking in any kind of gravitas... Hehe, a lady {i}does{/i} have to make herself desirable, after all... So we figured a game would be a nice way to spice it up.
{size=-5}And also finally make use of all those riddles we'd spent so much time cooking up.{/size}
We even prepared some rewards depending on how well you did!
Buuuut since your results were, like, kinda trash, no bonus goodies for you. We {i}are{/i} joining, though.
And don't worry, those unused rewards will simply get invested into your- {i}our{/i} town's enrichment. We're doing our part, too.♪
That's not too bad, I guess... At least, we got what we came here for. Still sucks to have almost completely flunked it, though...

The cherry slime remains a bit slumped with sadness at your near total defeat, prompting the three ghosts to joyfully hover with dance-like bobbing motions around the two of you, doing what they can to perk up their bummed-out host.
Don't be so glum, Perp! The important thing is you both had fun!~
And besides, we also got you this... Consider it a consolation prize.~
But you're shy of four correct answers...
Amanita speaks with stern authority, judgmentally crossing her arms and not hiding her disappointment.

Cackling dramatically, the three ghosts hover around the two of you, gesturing and grimacing in a way that is probably meant to be scary, but which instead comes off as harmlessly cute, given how much they can't hide their excitement, the show doing nothing to frighten Perpetua who ruefully sputters.
Wh- Guh- Dang it- W-We can try again, though, right?
No, for our trials are absooOooOluuUUte... But I do hope you had fun regardless, hihi.
I- {i}We{/i} weren't here just to have fun, though! Recruiting you into Sleim Vllge is serious business! Please, you have to let us try again- Tell them, [ThePlayerName]!!!
Grabbing your arm, the slime girl energetically shakes you, her unexpected distress making the riddling trio stop their giggling wails in surprise.
Wh-Whoah whaoh- Hold on- Calm down- It's okay-!
B-But that means all our efforts were in {i}vain{/i}! What am I going to tell Silver? And Seafoam? And-
A-Actually, that's what we wanted to say: we already decided we wanted to move in!
Yes indeed~ But it just felt too {i}easy{/i} and woefully lacking in any kind of gravitas... Hehe, so we figured a game would be a nice way to spice it up.
{size=-5}And also finally make use of all those riddles we'd spent so much time cooking up.{/size}
We even prepared some rewards depending on how well you did!
And you {i}did{/i} make an effort, so... Okay! We can give you a consolation prize!
Amanita nods magnanimously while Perpetua finally winds down from her panic attack, letting Virosa add comfortingly.
And don't worry, unused rewards will simply get invested into your- {i}our{/i} town's enrichment. We're doing our part, too.♪
That's a {i}lot{/i} better than I expected, given we didn't do that great... And I guess it {i}was{/i} pretty fun!
Riddles are the best path towards spiritual and intellectual enrichment.
Hehehe, as for the prizes, first for [ThePlayerName]...
Amanita floats over to you with a smile, reaching into her ectoplasmic body to take out a gleaming rune of some sort, which she ceremoniously puts into your hands.

Taking a bit of distance for privacy, the trio of ghostly slime women convene together in a huddle, whispering for a few minutes while Perpetua fidgets nearby, clearly anxious to know your result but not daring to interrupt your testers. Judging by the way they occasionally glance at you and giggle, though, you can't help but get the feeling that they're deliberately protracting the conclusion to ramp up the suspense...
Eventually, they turn back towards you and Perpetua with enigmatic smiles, but remain purposefully quiet, their silence urging the cherry goo girl to bite and ask.

In lieu of response, the sisters spring into action, erupting in festive cheers while coordinately tossing a shower of ghost-shaped confetti into the air with cheerful gusto, the papery shapes celebratorily flitting about under your surprised slimy partner's blinking eyes.
{b}Dun dun da dunnnn!{/b} Looks like you win!~
Yyyyup! Congratulations! You got at least four of the riddles correct.♪

Perpetua starts bouncing around in celebration while letting out giddy 'Ohoho!'s under the fond eyes of the trio, Virosa carrying on while stress visibly melts off your rambunctious partner in riddles.
And with that, I {i}suppose{/i} that means we {i}must{/i} relocate to your little village... Hehehe.♪

The ghostly monster girl's tone is full of mock 'Oh no, how could this happen to us?', though she quickly drops the pretense with an enthusiastic giggle while Perpetua's victory lap ends next to you, the slime girl practically vibrating with joy.

The overexcited and bouncy expedition leader leaps into you with a squishy and warm cherry-scented hug that makes her features wobble much like pudding for a brief second, her diminutive size thankfully posing no threat of knocking you over into the rocky surroundings.
While you enjoy the buzz of success, the riddling gang lines up ceremoniously, bowing in unison while Mycia and Amanita speak up in a singsong cadence.
We hope you had a lot of fun taking the stage.♪
It was quite the show of knowledge!♪
And in turn, as a reward for your sage mastery of language, we shall ooOOoffffer our patroooOOOoonaaaage.♪ Hihihihi-♪

Virosa makes sure to stress her final rhyming addition with a grand gesture of her arms that accompanies her raspy and spooky verbiage, the other two adding cackling laughter of their own while Perpetua slides off of you with a curious gaze at the quirky bunch.

Mycia explains with a giggle while Amanita drifts towards you with a smile, reaching into her ectoplasmic body to take out a duo of gleaming runes, which she ceremoniously puts into your hands under Perpetua's joyful grin.
Cool! It's almost like we delved into some old ruins and solved its trials!
You can keep them both, [ThePlayerName]. You'll get more use out of them than me.

We also had a little extra at the ready if you managed to get every riddle right... but I would not say it's anything to lose sleep over. And don't worry, unused rewards will simply get invested into your- {i}our{/i} town's enrichment.
While Perpetua grumbles, Amanita smoothly floats over to her with a cheerful grin of anticipation, making her look up in surprise.

But the wait is not long: without skipping a beat, the sisters spring into action, erupting in festive cheers while coordinately tossing a shower of ghost-shaped confetti into the air with cheerful gusto, the papery shapes celebratorily flitting about under your surprised slimy partner's blinking eyes.

Taken aback by the sudden flurry and stellar verdict, Perpetua just blankly stares at the trio as they continue to happily whoop and clap, her mind only catching up with reality when Amanita floats up to her to vigorously shake her hands.
Congratulations to you both! You got {i}every single question{/i} right.♪
The gooey red gal merrily jumps in place with Amanita, then does an exuberant fist pump with a hop, yelling out in excitement while the handshaking ghost moves from her to you.

Perpetua then starts bouncing around in celebration while letting out giddy 'Ohoho!'s under the fond eyes of the trio, Virosa carrying on while stress visibly melts off your rambunctious partner in riddles.

Is it really that much of a surprise, though? Feels like it was {i}quite{/i} easy to keep track of... Or did your nerves simply get to you, dear Perpetua? Hihihi... Then again, perhaps [ThePlayerName] is simply the brains of the outfit. He did do the heavy lifting, after all.~
{i}Buh-{/i} Sh-Shut up! Don't ruin the moment, spoilsport-!
The trio giggle playfully in unison at her outburst, Virosa raising her hands at the now pouting Perpetua in a gesture of peace.
I jest, I jest~ Of course, it was a team effort, and as agreed, in exchange for your {i}excellent{/i} performance, we will relocate to your quaint little village... Hehehe.♪
Gotta say, it will feel good to have more of a place of our own, instead of just a corner of the Caverns.
The warm reminder and comments renew Perpetua's enthusiasm, your companion literally vibrating with joy as she turns to you.

The overexcited and bouncy expedition leader leaps into you with a squishy and warm cherry-scented hug that makes her features wobble much like pudding for a brief second, her diminutive size thankfully posing no threat of knocking you over into the rocky surroundings.
While you enjoy the buzz of success, the riddling gang lines up ceremoniously, bowing in unison while Mycia and Amanita speak up in a singsong cadence.
We hope you had a lot of fun taking the stage.♪
It was quite the show of knowledge!♪
And in turn, as a reward for your sage mastery of language, we shall ooOOoffffer our patroooOOOoonaaaage.♪ Hihihihi-♪

Virosa makes sure to stress her final rhyming addition with a grand gesture of her arms that accompanies her raspy and spooky verbiage, the other two adding cackling laughter of their own while Perpetua slides off of you with a curious gaze at the quirky bunch.

Mycia explains with a giggle while Amanita drifts towards you with a smile, reaching into her ectoplasmic body to take out a trio of gleaming runes, which she ceremoniously puts into your hands under Perpetua's joyful grin.
Cool! It's almost like we delved into some old ruins and solved its trials!
You can have them, [ThePlayerName]. You'll get more use out of them than me.

These runes are our greatest treasure, so make good use of them, [ThePlayerName]~ But do not feel like you are dispossessing us. We will be sure to make {i}more{/i} treasures and cherished memories as we move forward and contribute to your- {i}our{/i} town's enrichment.
While Perpetua continues to glow with victorious excitement, Amanita smoothly floats over to her with a cheerful grin of anticipation, making her look up in sudden surprise.

With a deft flourish, Amanita reveals a hand-sized item: a figurine that sits atop a silver base, depicting the Black Knight in carefully immaculate detail, down to every individual joint of her gauntleted hand resting on the pommel of her red and black blade plunged into the stand, the sight leaving Perpetua gawking slack-jawed as she processes the wondrous object.

{i}Gfhgfawhaoahga-{/i} Th-That's a 'Darkest Night' limited-edition figurine of the Black Knight! Only {b}fifty{/b} copies were ever made, and she posed {b}herself{/b} for the sculpting! It's even said that she {i}personally{/i} bestowed her dark blessing upon each individual figurine, and that upon moonless nights, these artifacts may grant stygian dreams of eldritch might to their owner, instilling them with a spark of the Black Knight's majesty, spurring them on their path to rise to ever greater depths of power, her oneiric presence bolstering those who strive to support monsterkind!

She incoherently keeps babbling for a few moments, continuing to rapid-fire facts as she practically vibrates with glee, her overenthusiastic reaction looking to be actually scaring Amanita while Virosa chuckles.
{size=-5}Oh dear, I don't believe she can {i}think{/i} at all right now...~{/size}

After a while and more factoids and rumors about the Black Knight than you ever thought you'd hear in your life, Perpetua finally manages to get ahold of herself and slow down to a more intelligible pace, reverentially taking the figurine from Amanita in her own trembling gooey hands to look over carefully before exclaiming.

The spectral trio giggle happily at her reaction, only to let out flustered noises of surprise as she promptly leaps up and catches them in a tight group hug, dragging the floating monster girls closer to the ground in a squishy cluster.
{size=-5}I'm glad we don't need to breathe-{/size}
Ah- Though, {i}do{/i} be careful you don't drop it- Perhaps we should put it back in its box for now-?
The caution in Virosa's voice makes Perpetua's slimy lock of 'hair' atop her head stiffen in panic, the uberfan freeing the trio from her vice-like hug and nodding in agreement.
Y-Yes! That's a good idea! Wh-Where did you even get this- Why did you- I mean- I-I guess it's a bit rude to ask, s-so... Thank you so much-
Perpetua carefully clutches the figurine with tears of joy brimming in her eyes, Mycia and Virosa backpatting her dotingly while Amanita explains.
Well, since we first ran into you in the Caverns and you gave us your shy little offer of moving into your place, we've been mulling it over and looking into Sliem Villge...

Y-Yes, that's what I said... Anyways, as I said, we've been looking into the place. In case you didn't know, being {i}ghost{/i} slimes, we're {i}quite{/i} adept at going around unnoticed, which has enabled us to make observations {i}incognito{/i}...

As she explains with undisguised, shameless pride for what is essentially peeping, Amanita passes Perpetua a stark black box with {b}'DARKEST NIGHT'{/b} written on it in silver and red text, and together, they gently tuck the figure nice and safely into its confines, Perpetua dreamily hugging it as she no doubts thinks about where to store it among her adventuring belongings while Virosa continues.
... And with a little research and snooping, we found out your heart's desire, hihi.~ I'm glad we didn't miss the mark. {size=-5}Collector's items aren't cheap or easy to find...{/size}
O-Okay. That's a bit weird, but thank you again! Oh, this might just be the {i}best day ever{/i}-!
While she keeps cradling the box like a priceless treasure, Mycia's expression unexpectedly decomposes, the ghost slime gulping nervously.

While she keeps cradling the box like a priceless treasure, all thoughts of your unfortunate results completely forgotten, Mycia's expression unexpectedly decomposes, the ghost slime gulping nervously.
Uhm... Not to rain on anyone's parade or anything, but I have one more riddle for everyone... What's porcelain-white, purple, and sneaking up behind Perpetua-?
Amanita and Virosa look to Mycia with baffled confusion, then peer in the direction she's looking, their faces suddenly falling in turn while Perpetua frowns at them sternly.
Oh, come on, that's a trick as old as time and that's {i}not{/i} funny-

With the cherry slime rolling her eyes, you turn around together at the darkness behind her, revealing the stark white figure of Amy relaxedly looming there with an unreadable, fixed purple gaze on your party.

For the second time in short order, the cherry slime's words freeze in her mouth, albeit for a totally different reason, a deafening silence falling over all of you in the dark and claustrophobic Caverns. Amy, meanwhile, is not doing anything, just calmly sitting on her haunches with a curious look, yet you can feel the fragile and cracking tension build up with every passing second.

Then, with a simple, singular, slithery movement, Amy slightly sticks out her tongue from her mask-like visage, the peeking of the purplish organ accompanied by a small 'Blep' that instantly shatters the quietness as Perpetua flailingly freaks out.

Zooming with all the speed that her slimy body allows her, she rushes you and grabs you by the arm to drag you along with her down the tunnels, the ectoplasmic trio getting caught up in the red slime's infectious panic and reflexively zipping after her in flustered confusion. You don't think that anyone but you notices Amy letting out a playful chittering giggle.
{b}I told you Amy exists!{/b} {i}Run-Run-{b}Run{/b}-{/i} or she'll eat usss- Wait- We don't have legs, how do we ruuuun-
Mycie, c-calm down, she's not gonna {i}eat{/i} us- A-At worst, she'll just lick you like she did Perpetua, r-right?- And we {i}float-!{/i} Quickly!-

Wait, she {i}is{/i} real?- ...I thought it was just a Salaris running around covered in white paint to spook people- That's positively... incredible! A real eye-opener! In fact, that gives me an idea for a new riddle...

Not needing to really exert herself to move does wonders for Virosa's ability to speak eloquently on the move, the soliloquizing monster girl continuing to air out her thoughts while Perpetua incoherently huffs in panic as she leads you all down tunnel after tunnel, either relying on her memory or in blind panic... Hopefully the former.

Dragged along so suddenly by Perpetua's gooey grip and the slimes' shared panic, it takes a bit for your mind to catch up and realize you all probably didn't need to run away like this in the first place, Amy not even bothering to chase you...
However, by the time the thought strikes you, you can already see the checkpoint gem at the entrance to the Labyrinth, which your cherry leader dives for with a jubilant cry.
With a wet splat, she hits it face-first, and there's a blinding flash of light as you all pile together onto your destination-

And pop out in staggered unison near the entrance to Sleim Vllge, Perpetua messily picking herself up then standing proudly with a smug smile while the three ghosts look about blearily from the change of atmosphere and the starkness of the outdoors' luminosity, momentarily drifting about in a mild daze.
Nailed it- I saved us all, ohoho-

She then faceplants with a deflated whine as she lets go of your wrist and the exertion catches up all at once with her, the trio of ghosts letting out a sympathetic laugh at her plight and moving towards her to help her up...
... until a sudden breeze makes them all scrunch up with worry, the light gust sending their evanescent bodies drifting about with squeaks of panic.
Wh-While I would love to give you a break- could you please show us to our new home-?
Y-Yeah- Before we fall into the sky!
Y-You're not going to fall into the sky, dears... {size=-5}I'm pretty sure.{/size}

As Virosa sighs emphatically in exasperation, Perpetua hops back up triumphantly at her guests' eagerness to move on to their new home, pointing forward as she slimily scoots her way into the village.

As she leads them into the village proper, you're once again met by a number of lazy but cheerful waves from the local blue slimes, plus the one harpy who still seems to be on break. Merrily, the ghosts wave back, lightly chatting and exchanging greetings with the townsfolk, though Perpetua makes sure to keep your party moving without pause, lest you get caught in one delay after another.

Eventually, you get to what must be your destination, the landmark sticking out quite starkly amid Sleim Vllge's cheerfully pastoral, rustic landscape: a skull-shaped house whose maw gapes forebodingly, a set of double doors set into its mouth as if ready to be crushed between its teeth... Though you also can't help but find the proportions rather cute, the skeletal head looking to be grinning invitingly rather than snarling.

Regardless, the sight seems to be quite to the taste of the new Vllgers, the ghosts letting out spookily enthusiastic 'Oooh's and 'Aaah's as they take in the view while Perpetua proudly shows the way, opening the gate which groans ominously under her pull.
Oooh, it even creaks just the way I like it!♪

From the door, the entrance leads straight into an earthy expanse dug directly into a hillside, the interior looking quite like the Caverns, right down to glowing crystals probably harvested from there, albeit with more solid stonework and a variety of furniture for the slime ghosts to feel at home, including quite gothic-looking floor and wall coverings, a crystal-bearing chandelier, and even a large four-poster bed for them to sleep in.
Hmm, doesn't feel {i}that much{/i} different from our previous haunt...
I don't know, I can dig it!
There's even more than enough space for us to grow mushrooms.
Mh, well... I do suppose it is {i}quite{/i} adequate indeed.~
Together, the trio turn to you and Perpetua, and exclaim as one.
We'll just have to get our stuff from the Caverns, and we'll be good to go. Shouldn't take more than a few hours!
As the ghosts once more huddle up to make their plans, Perpetua turns to you.
All's well that ends well, [ThePlayerName], and we have you to thank for it! Who knew you were such a master riddle-answerer?♪
All's well that ends well, [ThePlayerName], and we have you to thank for it! I couldn't have done it without you!
All's well that ends well, [ThePlayerName].|f|ChangeImageLayer|/|Expression|/|1|/|Depressed|n|... To be honest, I can't help but think we could have done better, but still...
|f|ChangeImageLayer|/|Expression|/|1|/|Shy|n|Well, that was kinda rough, but it worked out in the end... I hope you weren't just goofing around, because...

It was nice to adventure with you, and I hope you enjoyed it too... Don't forget to check out Silver's shop when you have the time, by the way. I'm sure she'll have been able to make more wares available with our new friends!
For now, though, we should let them settle in.

The two of you walk out of the hillside house, closing the door behind you which slams shut with an ominous echoing noise, much to the delight of its new occupants judging by their muffled giddy cackling.
Well, that's where we'll be parting ways for now, [ThePlayerName]. Thank you again! I'll let you go back to your own adventures...
... while I'll go home and make sure to give this {i}jewel{/i} pride of place.♪

Giggling ecstatically, she caresses the 'DARKEST NIGHT' box which has thankfully made it out of the whole venture unscathed, then gives you a friendly wave before excitedly scooting her way back to her own house... Which now leaves you back to your own devices in the middle of Sleim Vllge after this escapade.

... Still, despite the ghost slimes' enthusiasm, you feel like they won't be {i}quite{/i} completely settled for the foreseeable future. You should probably come back at another point, once they've been able to get everything in order.
